label: When signing a lease for a premium apartment, what is your preferred legal structure?

options.label: Strictly individual contracts; I am only liable for my own room
A joint lease (solidary liability), but with a strict internal contract between us
I don't mind a joint lease if I trust the person I am moving in with

label: I would like to use a shared expenses app (e.g., Splitwise).
Are you comfortable securely storing a spare apartment key with a trusted neighbor or nearby family member in case of lockouts?

options.label: Yes
No, too much security risk

label: If we split the cost of a €800 living room sofa 50/50, and I move out after two years, what happens to the sofa?

options.label: You buy me out of my half based on its depreciated value
We sell it online and split the cash
I just leave it; the hassle of calculating depreciation isn't worth it

bipolarLabels.left: I have no idea how to do these things; I call someone
bipolarLabels.right: I am highly handy and take care of all physical maintenance myself

label: How do you handle routine apartment maintenance (e.g., bleeding radiators, replacing complex light fixtures, unclogging drains)?
I separate waste/recycling as required.
I'm comfortable with energy-saving practices (lights off, shorter showers).
Even if the landlord allows it, do you require a strict rule that no smoking (of any kind) occurs anywhere inside the accommodation, including leaning out windows?

options.label: Yes, strict outdoor smoking only
No, indoor smoking is negotiable

label: If a shared essential appliance (e.g., the washing machine) breaks and the landlord is not responsible, how do you handle a sudden €400 replacement cost?

options.label: I expect us to split it and pay for a new one immediately that same day
I would need a few weeks to budget for my half of the cost
I prefer it if we buy a cheap second-hand option to avoid high sudden costs.

label: I am highly meticulous about following strict building rules (e.g., exactly when to take out trash, not leaving bikes in the hallway, balcony regulations).

bipolarLabels.left: Subletting empty rooms can be useful

label: I expect a strict policy against subletting our rooms in any way or form.

bipolarLabels.left: I pay manually each month when I remember
bipolarLabels.right: Everything is set up via strict automated bank transfers

label: How do you handle your core monthly financial obligations (rent, major utilities)?
I agree to share basic emergency contact with housemates.
What is your current status and intent regarding pets in the apartment?

options.label: I have a pet and require a pet-friendly lease
I do not have a pet, but don't mind them
I do not have pets and prefer a pet-free apartment

label: If the landlord deducts €300 from our joint security deposit because your housemate damaged the flooring in the living room, what is your stance?

options.label: The housemate must reimburse me for the deduction completely
We split the loss 50/50, as we share the common spaces
I let it go; wear and tear is part of renting.

label: If you decide to leave a joint lease, how much internal notice do you believe you owe your housemate to find a replacement?

options.label: 1 month (the legal minimum)
2 months (standard courtesy)
3+ months (maximum stability and planning time)

label: I am highly organized and completely willing to have the main internet, water, and energy contracts registered in my personal name.
Do you legally require the ability to formally register yourself at this address with the municipality (Basisregistratie Personen)?

options.label: Yes, mandatory
No, not required

label: Do you intend to register a freelance business or LLC (e.g., KVK registration) at your home address?

bipolarLabels.left: Keep the house warm and split the high bill equally
bipolarLabels.right: Strictly monitor usage, turn down heating, and confront housemates who waste energy

label: How do you prefer to handle high gas/electricity prices in a shared apartment?

bipolarLabels.left: The leaving person finds a replacement entirely on their own
bipolarLabels.right: The staying person interviews and selects the replacement

label: If one of us moves out, how is the replacement process handled?
If a replacement roommate is needed, how much decision power should the remaining housemate have?

options.label: Final decision by remaining housemate
Joint decision (mutual veto)
Final decision by the person leaving

label: What is your intended timeline for living in your next apartment?

options.label: Short-term (6-12 months, e.g., a stepping stone)
Medium-term (1-2 years)
Long-term settlement (2+ years)

label: Do you believe the household should invest in shared liability/renter's insurance to protect our personal property in common areas?

options.label: Yes, it is necessary
No, individual insurance is fine

label: I am fully comfortable and capable of providing the standard income proofs (e.g., employer statements, salary slips showing 3x rent) required by premium housing agencies.
